done_for_real: TikTok affiliate upload API exists and imports real .xlsx files into module-local pipeline tables.
Raw staging and normalized facts are real and populated: tiktok_affiliate_import_batches=24, tiktok_affiliate_order_raw_staging=228171, content_order_facts=107988.
Attribution SQL exists at the locked business grain and uses TikTok last-touch selection in content_order_attribution.
Product/shop master tables exist and are populated: tt_product_master=401, tt_shop_master=279.
Cost input UI and isolated cost/profit schema exist.
Content library page loads real file-backed TikTok Studio registry data when the local registry is present.
visible_but_not_usable: Attribution pages exist but the broad query pattern times out on current real data volume.
Verification page exists but relies on the same unstable attribution surface for part of its checks.
Content library is visible but still contains placeholder actions and unassigned workflow states.
Profit page exists but live cost/allocation data is empty, so output is not business-usable.
partial_or_misleading: Upload UI claims idempotent re-upload behavior, but duplicate file hashes already exist across import batches.
Status filtering is broken because UI sends title-cased labels while DB stores lowercase normalized statuses.
Facts page still exposes legacy statuses Completed and Cancelled even though live facts only use settled, pending, awaiting_payment, and ineligible.
Data Health coverage metrics assume 100 percent when data exists and can misreport attribution availability.
Product/shop master tables exist in DB but runtime pages still aggregate directly from content_order_facts.
Several in-repo Content Ops docs still claim migration 102 is pending even though the tables now exist.
not_implemented: A real end-to-end operator flow of Upload Sales -> Map Product -> Link Video -> Add Cost -> See Profit.
A DB-backed video/content entity layer linked to sales, costs, and profit.
Preview-before-import for the Content Ops upload path.
Reject-bad-rows-before-write behavior for Content Ops imports.
Trustworthy live profit reporting with populated costs and allocations.
Retirement or isolation of the old order_attribution-based affiliate flow outside Content Ops.

architecture: Legacy order_attribution-based affiliate flow still coexists with the new content_order_attribution pipeline.
Runtime product/shop pages ignore persistent tt_product_master and tt_shop_master tables.
Video/content layer is still a separate file-backed viewer instead of a linked module entity layer.
data: content_order_attribution query pattern times out on real data volume.
Profit is not trustworthy because tt_content_costs and tt_content_cost_allocations are empty and summary data appears stale/manual.
In-repo docs are stale about migration 102 and no longer match the live DB.
flow: Status filters are broken across overview, orders, facts, product detail, and shop detail flows.
Content Ops import path lacks preview-before-import and validate-before-write behavior.
Duplicate file handling is weaker than the upload UI claims.
ui: Main operator console still preserves the dev-centric Facts -> Attribution -> Profit mental model.
Data Health presents confidence-sounding metrics that are not real coverage metrics.
Cost page copy does not match actual commission-first allocation logic.

target_gap.video: A file-backed Content Library exists, but it is not a DB-backed video/content system and is not linked into the sales-cost-profit operator flow.
target_gap.product_shop: Product and shop pages exist and product/shop master tables exist, but runtime pages still read from content_order_facts instead of the persistent master layer.
target_gap.sales_import: The technical import pipeline is real and populated, but it lacks preview-before-import, validate-before-write behavior, and truthful duplicate handling.
target_gap.cost: The cost table and UI exist, but no live costs are present and the operator guidance is slightly inconsistent with the DB allocation rules.
target_gap.profit: The profit schema and page exist, but live allocations are empty and the current summary output is not trustworthy for business use.

immediate_next_step.goal: Stabilize the Sales Import -> Facts -> Attribution truth path before any UX rebuild.
immediate_next_step.why: This is the narrowest change that most improves trustworthiness: it fixes broken status filters, stops treating attribution query failure as zero rows, and removes misleading import messaging without broad refactor.
